description: A designed comparison infographic in portrait format (identical reused asset to the agency's 'detainers' graphic posted on 2025-09-27). It uses a dark, gritty black asphalt/concrete textured background with a faint ghosted open metal handcuff in the lower portion. The headline in white sans-serif reads 'Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien:'. Two side-by-side columns appear under colored banners: a red banner 'UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' (left) and a blue banner 'COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' (right), separated by a vertical divider. Left column: a huge white numeral '8 to 10' with text 'ICE needs more officers to help protect the public from unpredictable behavior by an at-large criminal attempting to evade justice.' Right column: a large white '2' with text 'ICE can arrest a criminal alien with as few as two officers in a safe, controlled location, like a jail or court lockup area.' Bottom-left has the DHS/ICE seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'; bottom-right the URL 'ICE.gov/detainers'. The propaganda technique is a stark red-vs-blue numeric contrast arguing that non-cooperative/sanctuary jurisdictions force ICE to use far more officers and endanger the public, pressuring local governments to honor detainers. Target audience: the public and local officials.
notable_text: Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien: UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 8 to 10 / COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 2; ICE.gov/detainers
